title: net_profile (UNIX only)
text: net_profile (UNIX only) The net_profile settings defined in the Uniface ASN file helps in improving the security of the LDAP driver on UNIX. Arguments USYS$LDP = net_profile = <profile_name> Description In the driver settings string on UNIX, you can add the net_profile setting that points to a profile in the NET_SETTINGS. Note: The settings defined here applies strictly for UNIX. For adding settings on Windows, you must refer to the relevant Microsoft Windows website for details. Example In the following example, a profile in the NET_SETTING is defined and referred from the driver settings: Explain this code [DRIVER_SETTINGS] LDP = U3.0 USYS$LDP_PARAMS nulldefault=N, addbase=off, net_profile=”ldap_settings” Copy Explain this code [NET_SETTINGS] ldap_settings= verify_server=yes, verify_server_name=true, ca_certificate=/UNI1040120/ca-bundle.crt, min_tls_version=1.2,cipher_list=HIGH Copy Note: When a profile is defined, and one of the mentioned settings is found in the profile, all network related driver options are ignored. Also, when one of the network options is found in the network profile, the LDAP driver will switch to a TLS connection. Mappings in the settings The following table provides an overview of the existing driver settings and the network profile settings which replace them when a net_profile is used. When one of the options in the second column of the following table is found in the used network profile, the LDAP driver will switch to TLS and ignore the settings mentioned in the first column of the table. LDAP driver settings Net settings Open LDAP settings and descriptions tlscadir ca_location Open LDAP setting: LDAP_OPT_X_TLS_CACERTDIR Directory containing the CA certificates with hashed file names. Sets the path of the directories containing CA certificates. tlscafil ca_certificate Open LDAP setting: LDAP_OPT_X_TLS_CACERTFILESets/ The full-path of the CA certificate file. Min_tls_version LDAP_OPT_X_TLS_PROTOCOL_MIN 1.1 | 1.2 (default) Open LDAP does not yet support version 1.3. tls server validation = validate | ignore_name Verify_server = no (default) | yes Disable server certificate validation when set to “no”. Maps to the “validate” option when set to “yes”. tls server validation = validate|ignore_name Verify_server_name = no (default) | yes Maps to the “ignore_name” option when set to “no”. Cipher_list=<cipherlist> Defines the list of ciphers used in the connection, such as ALL , DEFAULT , LOW , MEDIUM , or HIGH The cipher_list can also point to a cipher for instance: cipher_list=TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA256 Related Links LDAP over TLS Was this topic helpful?
